deep_dive.title: Unified Context Evolution for LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: LLM-based agents can solve multi-step interactive tasks by combining reasoning with environment feedback, yet each episode starts from the same fixed context and any useful strategy discovered along the way is lost once the task ends. Existing approaches either limit learning to the current task or pool all experience into a single untyped store, without distinguishing knowledge types, tracking quality through use, or balancing what the library still lacks. We introduce Unified Context Evolution (UCE), a gradient-free framework that externalizes agent experience into an evolving library of typed Evolvable Context Units (ECUs). UCE decomposes experience into four complementary types (Memory, Strategy, Workflow, and Skill), each generated from trajectories under type-specific conditions, retrieved at decision time, scored through repeated usage outcomes, and pruned when no longer valuable. A scheduling module allocates each cycle's generation budget toward the types where the library is weakest. Across two interactive benchmarks, UCE raises ALFWorld success from 75.4% to 96.3% and WebShop task score from 45.1% to 61.3%, and the accumulated library transfers to alternative actor backbones without retraining.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Gradient-free framework that externalizes agent experience into an evolving library for post-training improvement
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无梯度框架，将智能体经验外部化为可进化的库，用于后训练改进
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无梯度框架，将智能体经验外部化为可进化的库，用于后训练改进
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 无梯度框架，将智能体经验外部化为可进化的库，用于后训练改进
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Unified Context Evolution (UCE), a gradient-free method for LLM agents to accumulate and refine typed experiences (Memory, Strategy, Workflow, Skill) over episodes, enabling continual improvement without retraining.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该文针对LLM智能体在多步交互任务中经验无法跨任务积累的问题，提出统一上下文进化框架UCE，将轨迹分解为记忆、策略、工作流和技能四种可进化上下文单元。通过类型化条件生成和质量追踪，智能体无需梯度更新即可持续优化表现，为智能体后训练提供了轻量级方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该文针对LLM智能体在多步交互任务中经验无法跨任务积累的问题，提出统一上下文进化框架UCE，将轨迹分解为记忆、策略、工作流和技能四种可进化上下文单元。通过类型化条件生成和质量追踪，智能体无需梯度更新即可持续优化表现，为智能体后训练提供了轻量级方案。

deep_dive.title: SkillPyramid: A Hierarchical Skill Consolidation Framework for Self-Evolving Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Recent AI agents can flexibly invoke skills to solve complex tasks, but their long-term improvement is fundamentally constrained by a lack of systematic skill construction, accumulation, and transfer. In particular, without a unified framework for skill consolidation, agents tend to redundantly construct similar capabilities across different tasks, are unable to effectively transform experience into reusable assets, and struggle to generalize task-specific skills to novel scenarios. To address this limitation, we propose SkillPyramid, a skill consolidation framework that reuses existing skill experience for broader task generalization. Operating on a hierarchical skill topology, SkillPyramid further introduces a self-evolution mechanism that enables agents to compose, validate, and incorporate new skills during task execution. Experiments on ALFWorld, WebShop, and ScienceWorld across four backbone models show that SkillPyramid substantially increases the average reward by 38.0% and reduces execution steps by 27.7%. Overall, our method transforms a skill collection from a static resource pool into a dynamic evolution system.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: self-evolving agents via hierarchical skill consolidation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自进化智能体的分层技能整合
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自进化智能体的分层技能整合
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自进化智能体的分层技能整合
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SkillPyramid, a hierarchical framework that consolidates skills for self-evolving agents, enabling continuous improvement by reusing experience and reducing redundant skill construction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对智能体长期提升中技能建设、积累和迁移缺乏系统框架的问题，提出SkillPyramid，通过构建分层技能拓扑并引入自进化机制，实现技能的整合与复用，使智能体能从经验中持续学习，提升任务泛化能力。实验表明该方法能有效减少冗余技能构建，增强跨任务适应性。

deep_dive.title: Learning While Acting: A Skill-Enhanced Test-Time Co-Evolution Framework for Online Lifelong Learning Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Lifelong learning is essential for Large Language Model (LLM) agents operating in dynamic, interactive environments. However, existing lifelong learning agents for long-horizon tasks typically depend on discrete skill or past experiences retrieval with static parameters during inference, which prevents them from continuously internalizing test-time feedback like human learners. To bridge this gap, we propose Skill-enhanced Test-Time Co-Evolution (\texttt{LifeSkill}), a two-stage reinforcement learning framework for Online Lifelong Learning Agents. Specifically, we design Verifier-Guided Skill Learning that addresses the lack of direct supervision for skill extraction by rewarding candidate skills according to the average verifier success of multiple skill-conditioned policy rollouts, encouraging the model to generate skills that are useful for solving tasks rather than merely plausible in text. Furthermore, we introduce Online Skill Internalization, which continuously improves the policy model during test-time interaction by transforming skill-conditioned trajectories into reward signals. This enables the agent to directly internalize reasoning capabilities into its parameters, avoiding the context bloat of experience retrieval. Experiments on LifelongAgentBench show that LifeSkill improves average performance by 7 absolute points by comparing with existing lifelong agent baselines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Two-stage RL framework for lifelong learning agents that extracts skills and co-evolves at test time, a post-training technique.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 两阶段强化学习框架，使代理提取技能并在测试时协同进化，是后训练技术。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 两阶段强化学习框架，使代理提取技能并在测试时协同进化，是后训练技术。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 两阶段强化学习框架，使代理提取技能并在测试时协同进化，是后训练技术。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: LifeSkill introduces a verifier-guided skill learning stage and a skill-conditioned co-evolution stage, enabling agents to continuously improve from test-time feedback without static parameters.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: LifeSkill提出验证器引导的技能学习阶段和技能条件下的协同进化阶段，使智能体能够从测试反馈中持续提升，无需静态参数。

deep_dive.title: Retrospective Harness Optimization: Improving LLM Agents via Self-Preference over Trajectory Rollouts
deep_dive.abstract: AI agents rely on a harness of skills, tools, and workflows to solve complex problems. Continually improving this harness is essential for adapting to new tasks. However, existing optimization methods typically require ground-truth validation sets, yet such labeled data is difficult to acquire in practical deployment settings. To address this problem, we introduce Retrospective Harness Optimization (RHO), a self-supervised method that optimizes the agent harness using only past trajectories. Specifically, RHO selects a diverse coreset of challenging tasks from past trajectories and re-solves them in parallel. The agent analyzes these rollouts using self-validation and self-consistency, then generates candidate harness updates and selects the most effective one by its own pairwise self-preference. We evaluate RHO across three diverse domains, spanning software engineering, technical work, and knowledge work. Notably, a single optimization round improves the pass rate on SWE-Bench Pro from 59% to 78% without any external grading. Furthermore, our analysis demonstrates that RHO effectively targets prior failure modes. As a result, the optimized harness alters the agent's behavior patterns and sustains higher accuracy during long-horizon sessions.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: self-supervised agent harness optimization from past trajectories
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自监督代理工具集优化基于历史轨迹
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自监督代理工具集优化基于历史轨迹
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自监督代理工具集优化基于历史轨迹
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Retrospective Harness Optimization (RHO), a self-supervised method that improves LLM agents by re-solving past challenging tasks and updating the harness based on self-validation and consistency, without needing labeled data.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对代理工具集优化缺乏标注数据的问题，提出回顾式优化方法RHO，从历史轨迹中选取挑战性任务并并行重解，通过自我验证和一致性分析生成工具集更新，实现无需人工标签的持续自我改进。实验表明该方法能有效提升智能体性能，降低人工干预需求。

deep_dive.title: ARBOR: Online Process Rewards via a Reusable Rubric Buffer for Search Agents
deep_dive.abstract: LLM-based search agents are trained predominantly with outcome-only reward, leaving the search process itself unsupervised. This signal degenerates on outcome-homogeneous groups where all sampled trajectories share the same correctness, yielding zero within-group advantage and no gradient. Existing process supervision either trains a costly verifier or generates per-query rubrics that are inconsistent across queries and discarded after one use. We propose ARBOR (Adaptive Rubric Buffer for Online Reward), a reusable process-reward framework that maintains a rubric memory shared across queries. Query-local drafts induced from contrastive trajectories are admitted, consolidated into cross-query common rubrics, and retired as the policy evolves. A small active subset of common rubrics scores trajectories via sparse pairwise judging, and the resulting scores are added to the base reward, providing process-level gradient even when outcome reward is uniform. ARBOR consistently outperforms GRPO and DAPO baselines on four multi-hop QA benchmarks, raising average LLM-judge accuracy by up to 4.2 points and converting up to 42% of otherwise-zero-gradient training groups into informative ones.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes a reusable process-reward framework for training LLM-based search agents via online reinforcement learning.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出可重用过程奖励框架，通过在线强化学习训练基于LLM的搜索智能体。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出可重用过程奖励框架，通过在线强化学习训练基于LLM的搜索智能体。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出可重用过程奖励框架，通过在线强化学习训练基于LLM的搜索智能体。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ARBOR addresses sparse outcome rewards in search agent training by maintaining a shared rubric buffer for process supervision, enabling more effective policy gradient when outcomes are homogeneous.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: ARBOR通过维护可跨查询共享的评分规则缓冲区，提供过程监督以解决搜索智能体训练中因结果同质化导致的稀疏奖励问题，从而在结果相同的情况下也能产生有效的策略梯度。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: ARBOR通过维护可跨查询共享的评分规则缓冲区，提供过程监督以解决搜索智能体训练中因结果同质化导致的稀疏奖励问题，从而在结果相同的情况下也能产生有效的策略梯度。

deep_dive.title: Can Generalist Agents Automate Data Curation?
deep_dive.abstract: Curating training data is among the most consequential yet labor-intensive parts of modern AI development: practitioners iteratively propose, implement, evaluate, and revise data policies against noisy benchmark feedback. We ask whether generalist coding agents can automate this data-curation loop. We introduce *Curation-Bench*, an agent-centric benchmark that fixes the model, training recipe, and evaluation suite while giving agents command-line access to inspect data, implement policies, submit them to a fixed training/evaluation pipeline, and revise. In a vision-language instruction-tuning instantiation, out-of-the-box agents reach strong published data-selection baselines within ten iterations. However, trajectory analysis reveals a persistent *execution-research gap*: agents mainly tune local policy variants rather than explore new policy families, even when given strategy guides and paper references. Scaffolds requiring each iteration to cite, instantiate, and adapt a prior method shift agents toward method-guided exploration. The scaffolded agent autonomously composes -- without human design input -- a data-selection policy that outperforms strong published baselines at one-tenth their data budget. Overall, current agents can run the curation loop, but reliable data research requires scaffolded method adaptation, not open-ended prompting alone. Code and benchmark are open-sourced.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: benchmarks generalist agents for automated data curation in vision-language instruction tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估通用智能体在视觉语言指令微调中自动化数据策管的能力
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估通用智能体在视觉语言指令微调中自动化数据策管的能力

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Curation-Bench tests coding agents on iterative data policy implementation for vision-language instruction tuning; agents match strong baselines but reveal an execution-research gap in revising data policies.

quick_skim.title: Coherence Maximization Improves Pluralistic Alignment
quick_skim.abstract: Aligning AI systems with diverse human values requires value specifications grounded in concrete examples, but generating such examples without extensive human supervision remains an open challenge. We investigate what makes these examples effective, using Internal Coherence Maximization (ICM) -- which infers labels by maximizing their mutual predictability -- to generate persona-specific examples that steer a model toward a target group's values, without human supervision. Across four benchmarks spanning classification, preference, and open-ended generation, ICM-inferred in-context examples match the performance of gold labels. Crucially, coherence matters beyond individual label accuracy: with accuracy held constant, more coherent examples generalize substantially better than incoherent ones. For personas underrepresented in pretraining data, targeted human feedback on the questions where the model is least certain about a persona's values yields better generalization than the same number of labels on arbitrary questions. These results identify coherence as a key design principle for scalable value specification, leveraging the diverse human perspectives already encoded in pretrained language models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: unsupervised generation of alignment examples via coherence maximization, applicable to aligning any agent, including financial ones
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 无监督对齐示例生成，可应用于金融智能体对齐
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 无监督对齐示例生成，可应用于金融智能体对齐
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 无监督对齐示例生成，可应用于金融智能体对齐
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes using Internal Coherence Maximization to generate persona-specific alignment examples without human supervision, enabling pluralistic alignment of models to diverse values, with potential application to financial AI agents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对AI系统与多元人类价值观对齐中标注数据昂贵的问题，提出利用一致性最大化从少量示例中推断标签，生成符合特定群体价值观的样本，实现无需人工监督的个性化对齐。该方法在多个基准上达到黄金标签水平，且连贯性高的样本能显著提升泛化能力。这为金融智能体等场景提供了高效的对齐手段。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对AI系统与多元人类价值观对齐中标注数据昂贵的问题，提出利用一致性最大化从少量示例中推断标签，生成符合特定群体价值观的样本，实现无需人工监督的个性化对齐。该方法在多个基准上达到黄金标签水平，且连贯性高的样本能显著提升泛化能力。这为金融智能体等场景提供了高效的对齐手段。
